info.title: Owlery Developer Portal API
info.description: The API URL is `https://api.owlery.ai/api/ext/v1`. Access is handled via an API key passed as Bearer Token. Contact `support@owlery.ai` for an API Key. Endpoints are grouped by audience: shipper-facing APIs, carrier-facing APIs, and facility-facing APIs.

tags.name: shipper-plan
tags.description: Create transportation plans for one or more orders.

tags.name: shipper-documents
tags.description: Search and download documents attached to loads and orders.

tags.name: shipper-items
tags.description: Create, update, or delete inventory items.

tags.name: shipper-rate-quote
tags.description: Request freight rate quotes from existing orders or standalone quote data.

tags.name: shipper-tender
tags.description: Tender a specific rate quote response at its quoted price.

tags.name: shipper-webhooks
tags.description: Webhooks sent by Owlery when orders, tenders, invoices, or order-level load stop dates are updated.

tags.name: carrier-tracking
tags.description: Push load tracking status updates to Owlery.

tags.name: carrier-rate-quotes
tags.description: Submit carrier quote responses asynchronously to Owlery.

tags.name: carrier-tender-responses
tags.description: Carrier responses to original tenders and tender edits.

tags.name: carrier-webhooks
tags.description: Rate quote, tender, tender edit, and cancel webhooks sent by Owlery to carrier systems. Authentication is provided through carrier-configured request headers, not body fields.

tags.name: facility-orders
tags.description: Confirm one or more orders, updating their status and items.

post.description: This endpoint creates a transportation plan for one or more orders.

Webhook payload sent when orders are updated. Webhooks are sent when orders are marked as `closed` and no webhook has been sent previously (unless manually re-triggered). Orders reach `closed` status through: (1) Owlery API Order Confirmation via `/api/ext/v1/order/confirm`, (2) EDI Transactions (944/945), or (3) Integration Sync (e.g. WMS).

Webhook payload sent when tenders are created, accepted, or BOLs are generated. Tender webhooks are sent when a tender reaches one of the following statuses: BOL Created, Carrier Selected, or Tendered. The `loadNumber` field is optional at this stage: some carriers do not return a load number until the tender is accepted. Before acceptance, Owlery only sends `loadNumber` when a real load number is already available, or when Universal Shipment ID enforcement is enabled for a supported Manual/EDI-style tender and the carrier/source tender identifier is intentionally used as the Owlery load number.

consolidatedTender.summary: Consolidated tender with header-level order mapping

multiOrderDifferentStopIndexes.summary: Multiple orders with different route and stop-type indexes
multiOrderDifferentStopIndexes.description: Demonstrates that pickupStopIndex/dropOffStopIndex are route stop indexes, while pickupSequenceNumber/dropoffSequenceNumber are zero-based within pickup or dropoff stops only.

400.description: Receiver rejected the webhook as invalid. Owlery logs the failed delivery and does not mark that payload as sent.

401.description: Receiver rejected the webhook because custom authentication headers were missing or invalid. Owlery logs the failed delivery and does not mark that payload as sent.

value.message: Invalid webhook signature or API key

500.description: Receiver failed while processing the webhook. Owlery logs the failed delivery and does not mark that payload as sent.

post.description: Webhook payload sent when a tendered order's related load stop estimated, appointment, or actual dates are first populated or later changed. This includes the initial tender when those load dates first become available. The payload is order-based: each stop `index` is zero-based within the order itself, not within the underlying load, so the index is `0` for pickup and `1` for dropoff even when the load is consolidated across more stops. Owlery only sends this webhook for orders it tendered, because Owlery must have the order-to-load stop mapping from the tender/BOL flow.

example.message: Order load stop dates updated successfully

400.description: Receiver rejected the webhook as invalid. Owlery logs the failed delivery.

401.description: Receiver rejected the webhook because custom authentication headers were missing or invalid. Owlery logs the failed delivery.

value.message: Invalid webhook signature or API key

500.description: Receiver failed while processing the webhook. Owlery logs the failed delivery.

value.message: Unable to process order load stop date webhook

post.description: Webhook payload sent when invoices are created or updated in Owlery. Sent when invoices are created or modified, when invoice status changes (e.g., from unpaid to paid), or when invoices are associated with loads.

lineItems.description: Line haul from Chicago to New York

post.description: This endpoint allows brokers to submit invoices directly to Owlery. The source system is determined from the broker profile associated with the API key.

lineItems.description: Line haul from Chicago to New York

post.description: This endpoint confirms one or more orders, updating their status and items.

207.description: Partial success - some orders failed to confirm

post.description: This endpoint allows brokers to create one or more loads in Owlery using the standardized external payload.

207.description: Partial success - some loads failed validation

failures.error: Stop 0 orderDates.startAt must be a valid ISO8601 date

post.description: Push tracking status of a load to Owlery. The `at` field represents when the tracking event occurred or when the status changed (event metadata). For `AppointmentScheduled` status, the `appointment` field contains the actual appointment time range.

207.description: Partial success - some load numbers not found

post.description: Accept a tender or tender edit that Owlery sent to the carrier. Identify the shipment using the Owlery `loadNumber` from the carrier tender or tender edit webhook.

200.description: Tender or tender edit accepted successfully

post.description: Reject a tender or tender edit that Owlery sent to the carrier. Identify the shipment using the Owlery `loadNumber` from the carrier tender or tender edit webhook.

200.description: Tender or tender edit rejected successfully

post.description: Webhook sent by Owlery to a carrier when Owlery requests rates. The request body itself is the rate quote request. Owlery sends carrier-configured authentication headers with this request. The carrier should use those headers to identify the shipper/account context. Return `200 OK` to acknowledge receipt only. Submit all quote responses asynchronously to `POST /api/ext/v1/rate-quote/responses`; Owlery does not process quote results from the webhook response body.

200.description: Webhook acknowledged. Do not return quote data in this response; submit all quote responses asynchronously to Owlery.

400.description: Receiver rejected the webhook as invalid. Owlery logs the failed delivery and may retry according to the configured delivery policy.

401.description: Receiver rejected the webhook because authentication headers were missing or invalid.

example.message: Invalid webhook signature or API key

500.description: Receiver failed while processing the webhook. Owlery logs the failed delivery and may retry according to the configured delivery policy.

post.description: Webhook sent by Owlery to tender a shipment to a carrier. Owlery sends `loadNumber`; carriers should use that Owlery load number when accepting or rejecting the tender and when later referencing tracking, invoices, and documents for this load. Owlery sends carrier-configured authentication headers with this request. The carrier should use those headers to identify the shipper/account context.

400.description: Receiver rejected the webhook as invalid. Owlery logs the failed delivery and may retry according to the configured delivery policy.

401.description: Receiver rejected the webhook because authentication headers were missing or invalid.

example.message: Invalid webhook signature or API key

500.description: Receiver failed while processing the webhook. Owlery logs the failed delivery and may retry according to the configured delivery policy.

post.description: Webhook sent by Owlery when a tendered load changes. This same payload is used for load edits after the carrier already knows the load. The payload is a full replacement view of the current tender/load details; carriers should update their shipment using `loadNumber` as the identifier. If the carrier must accept or reject the edit, call the existing accept or reject endpoint with that same `loadNumber`.

400.description: Receiver rejected the webhook as invalid. Owlery logs the failed delivery and may retry according to the configured delivery policy.

401.description: Receiver rejected the webhook because authentication headers were missing or invalid.

example.message: Invalid webhook signature or API key

500.description: Receiver failed while processing the webhook. Owlery logs the failed delivery and may retry according to the configured delivery policy.

post.description: Webhook sent by Owlery to cancel a tender or load previously sent to the carrier. The payload includes Owlery `loadNumber`; carriers should use that value to identify the shipment.

400.description: Receiver rejected the webhook as invalid. Owlery logs the failed delivery and may retry according to the configured delivery policy.

401.description: Receiver rejected the webhook because authentication headers were missing or invalid.

example.message: Invalid webhook signature or API key

500.description: Receiver failed while processing the webhook. Owlery logs the failed delivery and may retry according to the configured delivery policy.

post.description: Search for loads using reference numbers.

get.description: Search documents attached to loads and orders in the authenticated organization. If `searchText` is shorter than 2 characters, the response is an empty result set.

parameters.name: searchText
parameters.description: Search term (min 2 characters to return results).

example.errorMessage: Search query is too broad. Please refine your search criteria to get more specific results.

get.description: Download a single document by ID. The response is a file stream with download headers.

post.description: This endpoint allows shippers to create or update one or more inventory items in Owlery. When the SKU already exists, the item with the same SKU will be updated.

200.description: All items created or updated successfully

put.description: This endpoint allows shippers to update an existing inventory item by its SKU.

example.error: Item with SKU A01-COFFEE not found

delete.description: This endpoint allows shippers to delete an inventory item by its SKU.

example.message: Item with SKU A01-COFFEE deleted successfully

example.error: Item with SKU A01-COFFEE not found

post.summary: Create Rate Quote from Existing Order
post.description: Request a freight rate quote using an existing order. Either `orderNumber` or `orderReferenceNumber` is required.

get.description: Retrieve a rate quote and all of its carrier responses by short ID.

200.description: Rate quote details with carrier responses

post.description: Request freight rate quotes without an existing order. At least one `pickup` and one `dropoff` stop are required. Returns the rate quote ID and any responses received synchronously from integrations.

200.description: Rate quote created with carrier responses

example.error: At least one pickup stop is required

post.description: Tender (accept) a specific rate quote response at its quoted price. This initiates the booking with the carrier. Returns the updated tender status and load number if one was created.

get.description: Retrieve the current tender status for a rate quote response.

parameters.name: rateQuoteResponseId
parameters.description: The rate quote response ID to look up.

post.description: Submit carrier rate quote responses asynchronously to Owlery after receiving a Rate Quote Requested webhook. Use the `id` from the webhook request and return it as `id` in this submission. Submit as many modes, service options, and prices as possible in one call; Owlery creates one quote response per item.

202.description: Rate quote responses accepted for processing

BearerAuth.description: API key passed as Bearer Token. Contact support@owlery.ai for an API Key.

CarrierWebhookAuth.name: Authorization
CarrierWebhookAuth.description: Authentication headers sent by Owlery to carrier webhook endpoints. The exact header name and value are configured per carrier, and may be a bearer token or any other static header the carrier can validate. The carrier should use these authentication headers to identify the shipper/account context. Owlery does not send shipper organization IDs, shipper profile IDs, or account routing identifiers in the webhook body.

isTest.description: Whether or not the order is a test order.

orderNumber.description: The unique number of the order.

source.description: The system the order originates from. Email support@owlery.ai to add new sources.

sourceId.description: The unique ID of the order from the source system. This can be the same as orderNumber if a separate unique ID is not available.

stops.description: The stops associated with the order.

items.description: The items being shipped as part of the order.

index.description: The zero-based index of the stop.

preventSendOrder.description: Whether or not we should not attempt to send the order to this facility.

referenceNumbers.description: Reference numbers specific to the stop.

lotCode.description: The lot code of the item.

expectedQuantity.description: The expected quantity of the item.

expirationDate.description: The expiration date of the item.

orderNumber.description: The unique number of the order.

isTest.description: Whether or not the order is a test order.

source.description: The system the order originates from.

sourceId.description: The unique ID of the order from the source system.

items.description: The items being shipped as part of the order.

expectedQuantity.description: The expected quantity of the item.

actualQuantity.description: The actual quantity of the item.

sourceId.description: The unique identifier of the item from a source system.

lotCode.description: The lot code of the item.

actualQuantity.description: The actual quantity of the item.

expirationDate.description: The expiration date of the item.

sourceId.description: The unique identifier of the item from a source system.

loadNumber.description: Load number associated with the tender, when available. For tender events before carrier acceptance, this may be omitted because some carriers only provide the load number on acceptance. If Universal Shipment ID enforcement is enabled for a supported Manual/EDI-style tender, Owlery may send the source tender ID here because that identifier is used as the Owlery load number.

referenceNumbers.description: Reference numbers associated with the tender.

isTeamRate.description: Whether this is a team driver rate.

isContractRate.description: Whether this is a contract rate from a rate on file.

isDirectTender.description: Whether this is a direct tender.

stops.description: Array of tender stops with zero-based route index, facility/date information, freight items, and associated order numbers.

orders.description: Order-level mapping to pickup/dropoff route stops and zero-based stop-type sequence numbers.

updatedAtInMs.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the tender status was updated.

updatedBy.description: Email address of the user who created the tender.

index.description: Zero-based stop index from the rate quote route.

orders.description: Order numbers associated with this stop.

items.description: Freight items associated with this stop.

orderNumber.description: Order number associated with the tender stop.
Order number associated with the tender.

pickupStopIndex.description: Zero-based index of the route stop where this order is picked up.

dropOffStopIndex.description: Zero-based index of the route stop where this order is dropped off.

pickupSequenceNumber.description: Zero-based sequence of the pickup stop among pickup stops only.

dropoffSequenceNumber.description: Zero-based sequence of the dropoff stop among dropoff stops only.

lookupCode.description: Internal lookup code for the facility. At least one of this or (source + sourceId) must be provided.

sourceId.description: Unique identifier of the facility in the source system. At least one of lookupCode or (source + sourceId) must be provided.

startAt.description: Start date in ISO8601 format (without time).

endAt.description: End date in ISO8601 format (without time).

email.description: The email address of the contact.

phone.description: E.164 format of the phone number.

type.description: The type of the reference number.

value.description: The value of the reference number.

failures.description: Failure messages for orders which were not upserted successfully.

orderNumber.description: The unique number of the order.

items.description: The items being confirmed as part of the order.

isTest.description: Whether this is a test order.

sourceId.description: The unique ID of the item in the source system. Such as the line number.

actualQuantity.description: The actual quantity of the item.

damagedQuantity.description: The damaged quantity of the item.

lotCode.description: The lot code of the item.

expirationDate.description: The expiration date of the item (for outbound orders only).

confirmedOrderNumbers.description: Array of order numbers that were successfully confirmed.

errors.description: Array of error messages for orders that failed to be confirmed.

loadNumber.description: Owlery load number from the carrier tender webhook. Carriers should use this Owlery load number when submitting invoices.

dueAt.description: Payment due date in ISO8601 format.

isTest.description: Whether this is a test invoice (default: false).

total.description: Total price for this line item in cents.

accountId.description: Account identifier for the bill-to party.

loadNumbers.description: Array of load numbers associated with this invoice. Can contain multiple loads for consolidated invoices.

dueAt.description: Payment due date in ISO8601 format.

orderNumber.description: The unique number of the order.

loadNumber.description: Load number whose stop dates changed.

source.description: The source system the order originates from, such as NetSuite.

sourceId.description: The unique order identifier from the source system.

stops.description: Changed order stops. Stop indexes are zero-based within the order, not within the load.

index.description: Zero-based stop index within the order. For a standard two-stop order, pickup is `0` and dropoff is `1`, even if the underlying load has additional consolidated stops.

dateTime.description: ISO8601 timestamp for the date/time value.

timeZone.description: IANA time zone identifier for the stop.

isDateOnly.description: Whether the value should be interpreted as a date without a specific time.

loadNumber.description: Owlery load number from the carrier tender webhook. Carriers should use this Owlery load number when submitting tracking updates.

at.description: The timestamp when this tracking event occurred or when the status changed (event metadata). This field is always required. For `AppointmentScheduled` status, this is when the appointment was scheduled, NOT the appointment time itself (use `appointment` field for that).

stopIndex.description: The zero-based stop index of the associated status update. Required for stop-specific statuses: AppointmentScheduled, ArrivedAtPickup, LoadingBegin, PickedUp, ArrivedAtDropoff, UnloadingBegin, and DroppedOff.

startAt.description: Start date and time of the appointment window in ISO8601 format.

endAt.description: End date and time of the appointment window in ISO8601 format.

success.description: Array of load numbers that were successfully processed.
Array of load numbers that were successfully processed.

missing.description: Array of load numbers that could not be found in the system.

referenceNumbers.description: Array of reference number values to search for.

loads.description: Array of loads matching the search criteria.

loadNumber.description: The unique identifier of the load.

referenceNumbers.description: Reference numbers associated with the load.

stops.description: Array of stops in the load's route.

brokerProfiles.description: Deprecated: Use `brokerProfile` instead. Array of brokers associated with the load.

trackingNumber.description: Tracking number used for carrier API tracking.

isDateOnly.description: Whether this represents a date without time.

scac.description: Mode-specific SCAC code of the broker.

loads.description: Array of loads to create in a single request. All loads are validated before processing.

createdLoads.description: Loads that passed validation and were persisted.

failures.description: Validation or processing failures per load number.

loadNumber.description: The load number that was accepted.
The load number that failed validation. May be omitted if the payload lacked a load number.
Unique load identifier. Must be unique within the request body.

referenceNumbers.description: Reference numbers at the load level.

stops.description: Ordered list of stops (must contain at least one pickup and one dropoff).

liveDropType.description: Live/drop classification. Defaults to `Live` when omitted.

orderDates.description: Planned order date. Must be a valid ISO8601 value; the time component is optional (e.g., `2024-01-01`).

appointmentDates.description: Appointment date. Must be a valid ISO8601 value; the time component is optional.

estimatedDates.description: Estimated date/time. Must be a valid ISO8601 value; the time component is optional.

actualDates.description: Actual date/time. Must be a valid ISO8601 value; the time component is optional.

lookupCode.description: Internal lookup code for the facility.

countryCode.description: ISO-3166-1 Alpha-2 country code. If omitted, the system will automatically infer the country code based on the state code.

isStackable.description: Whether the shipment can be stacked.

isTurnable.description: Whether the shipment can be turned.

Carrier.description: Carrier identifiers used to attach an internal carrier profile. At least one of DOT, MC, or SCAC must be provided.

page.description: 0-based page number of the current response.

errorMessage.description: Error message when the search fails.

size.description: File size in bytes when available.

items.description: Array of inventory items to create.

upsertedItems.description: Items that were successfully created or updated.

failures.description: Validation or processing failures per item.

sku.description: The SKU of the item that was created.
The SKU of the item that failed. May be omitted if the payload lacked a SKU.
Unique stock keeping unit identifier. When using the Update Item by SKU API, this field will be ignored.

isStackable.description: Whether the item can be stacked.

isTurnable.description: Whether the item can be turned.

parentType.description: The parent packaging type in the hierarchy (e.g., Case is parent of Each).

quantityPerParent.description: Number of this unit that fit in one parent unit (e.g., 10 Eaches per Case). Required if parentType is provided.

orderNumber.description: Order number to quote. Either this or `orderReferenceNumber` is required.

orderReferenceNumber.description: Reference number of the order to quote. Either this or `orderNumber` is required.

additionalReferenceNumbers.description: Additional reference numbers to attach to the quote.

additionalPickupReferenceNumbers.description: Reference numbers to add to the pickup stop.

additionalDropoffReferenceNumbers.description: Reference numbers to add to the dropoff stop.

shortId.description: Short human-readable ID for the rate quote.

stops.description: Ordered list of stops. Must contain at least one Pickup and one Dropoff.

referenceNumbers.description: Reference numbers at the quote level.

externalCustomerReferenceNumbers.description: Reference numbers identifying the external customer associated with the quote.

items.description: Items being shipped at this stop.

isStackable.description: Whether the item can be stacked.

isTurnable.description: Whether the item can be turned.

sourceId.description: Unique ID of the facility in the source system.

shortId.description: Short human-readable ID for the rate quote.

responses.description: Carrier responses received. May be empty if no integrations responded yet.

shortId.description: Short human-readable ID for the rate quote.

externalCustomerReferenceNumbers.description: Reference numbers identifying the external customer associated with the quote.

responseId.description: Unique ID of this carrier response. Used for tendering.

expiresAt.description: ISO8601 date after which this quote is no longer valid.

stops.description: Stop-level details returned by the carrier.

cents.description: Price in the smallest currency unit (e.g. cents for USD).

isDateOnly.description: Whether this represents a date without a specific time.

rateQuoteResponseId.description: The ID of the rate quote response to tender. Obtained from the `responseId` field of a rate quote response.
The rate quote response ID that was queried.

loadNumber.description: Load number associated with this tender, if a load has been created.
Owlery load number from the carrier tender or tender edit webhook.
Owlery load number from the carrier tender or tender edit webhook.
Owlery load number associated with this tender or tender edit.

id.description: Configured external account identifier for routing on the carrier side. This is not an Owlery internal organization or shipper profile ID.
Carrier-facing Owlery rate quote ID. Return this value as `id` when submitting quote responses to Owlery.

stops.description: Pickup and dropoff stops for the quote request.

items.description: Quote-level freight items, if not already provided on stops.

id.description: Carrier-facing Owlery rate quote ID from the Rate Quote Requested webhook. This value must be returned when submitting quote responses.

responses.description: Carrier quote responses. Submit as many modes, service options, and prices as possible.

carrierQuoteId.description: Carrier-generated unique ID for this quote. Owlery sends this value back during tender.

mode.description: Transportation mode for this price. Submit separate response objects for different modes, service levels, or prices.

price.description: Total all-in price for this quote unless `allInRate` is explicitly false.

lineItems.description: Charge breakdown in cents. Discounts should be sent as negative line items.

carrier.description: Carrier details. Required when `mode` is `LTL`; `scac` is required when carrier is provided.

interline.description: For LTL responses, whether the service uses an interline carrier.

allInRate.description: Whether `price` is an all-in total. Defaults to true when omitted.

expiresAt.description: ISO8601 timestamp after which this quote is no longer valid.

stops.description: Stop-level estimated or appointment dates, if available.

id.description: Carrier-facing Owlery rate quote ID from the Rate Quote Requested webhook.

carrierQuoteId.description: Carrier quote ID returned when submitting rate quote responses.

loadNumber.description: Owlery load number. Use this value when later referencing tracking, invoices, and documents for this load.

stops.description: Tender stops. Facility location must include street, city, state, zipcode, and countryCode. Reference numbers are not included before carrier acceptance.

items.description: Tender freight items, if not already provided on stops.

id.description: Carrier-facing Owlery rate quote ID from the Rate Quote Requested webhook.

carrierQuoteId.description: Carrier quote ID returned when submitting rate quote responses.

loadNumber.description: Owlery load number. Use this value when later referencing tracking, invoices, and documents for this load.

stops.description: Tender stops. Facility location must include street, city, state, zipcode, and countryCode. Reference numbers are not included before carrier acceptance.

items.description: Tender freight items, if not already provided on stops.

liveDropType.description: Whether the stop is a live load/unload or a drop trailer stop. Defaults to `live` when omitted.

items.description: Items being shipped at this stop.

index.description: Zero-based index of the stop from the rate quote request.

type.description: Stop type from the rate quote request.

liveDropType.description: Whether the stop is a live load/unload or a drop trailer stop.

id.description: Carrier-facing Owlery rate quote ID submitted by the carrier.

accepted.description: Number of rate quote response items accepted by Owlery.

name.description: Name of the carrier, if available.

total.description: Line item amount in cents. Discounts should be sent as negative numbers.

level.description: Guaranteed delivery commitment. Omit this object for standard/non-guaranteed service.

liveDropType.description: Whether the stop is a live load/unload or a drop trailer stop. Defaults to `live` when omitted.

items.description: Items being shipped at this stop.

loadNumber.description: Owlery load number for the tender/load being edited. Use this value when accepting or rejecting the edit and when later referencing tracking, invoices, and documents.

stops.description: Updated tender/load stops. Treat this as the current full stop list. Facility location must include street, city, state, zipcode, and countryCode.

items.description: Updated tender/load freight items, if not already provided on stops.
